project_title: Quantitative Capstone - quantitative arm of the AI-corruption capstone study
product_name: Quantitative Capstone - quantitative arm of the AI-corruption capstone study
runtime_target: packet registry, routing table, and promotion decision board
operational_end_state: A reviewer can see source identity, authority, route, output impact, and next build decision.
full_build_definition: A research packet with question, method, evidence, source utilization, analysis plan, limitations, artifact outputs, review gates, and submission or conference-readiness criteria.
minimum_operational_release: A reviewable research packet for Quantitative Capstone - quantitative arm of the AI-corruption capstone study: research question, design, evidence map, analysis plan, limitations, source-use table, and next empirical or publication step.
required_modules_to_build: source registry | output mapping | claim boundary | close-read queue
source_status: 6 source-application rows; 1 active passage contracts; 0 unlocated source candidates.
source_actions_before_claim: Complete 1 close-read contract(s), update 3 linked proof-capsule field(s), and preserve the current claim ceiling until passage notes exist.
test_suite: claim ceiling test: public claims cannot exceed built evidence | source test: metadata-routed sources require close reading before citation or quotation | privacy test: no local paths or private directories in public deploy
acceptance_tests: Verify that the question, design, evidence, analysis plan, limitations, and source use support only the claims currently made.
promotion_stage: full-build candidate; execute minimum operational release next
current_status: source-backed proof build; needs source-to-output capture and close-read strengthening
launch_or_review_path: Open the project contract for `row1`, assemble the research packet, and run source, method, and claim-boundary checks.
first_full_build: Add method-result boundary, validity threats, what the study can and cannot generalize, and a practitioner-facing implication.
next_implementation_steps: 1. create or confirm runtime data schema; 2. implement one end-to-end fixture; 3. capture proof artifact; 4. run source and claim-ceiling gates; 5. publish reviewer-safe demo
public_claim_now: May claim a full-build candidate exists with proof and source contracts; do not claim deployment, outside validation, or production readiness until live execution and passage verification are recorded.
claim_boundary: This is a full-build plan, not a claim that the full operational system has already been completed.
